data.description:
## Why

`pyproject.toml`'s `[project]` table has `name`, `version`, `description`,
`readme`, `requires-python`, and `license` — but no `authors`, `maintainers`,
`keywords`, `classifiers`, or `[project.urls]`. For a package meant to be adopted
as a CLI, MCP server, and library, that metadata IS distribution UX: it drives
PyPI discovery, maintainer attribution, maturity signaling, and the sidebar links
users rely on.

## What

Add to `pyproject.toml` `[project]` (values locked with the maintainer):

- `authors = [{name = "Nava PBC", email = "hello@navapbc.com"}]` and a
  `maintainers` entry for the named maintainer (Joe Oakhart,
  `joeoakhart@navapbc.com`).
- `keywords = ["mcp", "cli", "jira", "agent-coordination", "ticketing",
  "event-sourcing", "git"]`.
- `classifiers`: `Development Status :: 4 - Beta`; `Intended Audience ::
  Developers`; `License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License`;
  `Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11` + `:: 3.12` (matches the CI matrix);
  `Operating System :: POSIX` + `:: MacOS`; `Topic :: Software Development ::
  Bug Tracking` + `:: Version Control :: Git`.
- `[project.urls]`: `Homepage` + `Repository` -> the GitHub repo;
  `Documentation` -> the repo `docs/`; `Issues` -> the GitHub issues URL;
  `Changelog` -> `docs/release-notes.md`; `Security` -> `SECURITY.md`.

## Scope

`pyproject.toml` only. Do not change `name`, `version`, dependencies, or extras.
Keep classifiers consistent with the real CI Python matrix (3.11/3.12). The
`Security` URL points at the file delivered by the community-health story.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `authors` + `maintainers` populated as above.
- [ ] `keywords` and `classifiers` present; Dev Status is `4 - Beta`; Python
  classifiers match `requires-python`/CI (3.11, 3.12).
- [ ] `[project.urls]` includes Homepage, Repository, Documentation, Issues,
  Changelog, Security.
- [ ] `python -m build` (or the packaging metadata check) succeeds and the
  rendered metadata shows the new fields; `make lint` stays green.
data.title: Enrich pyproject package metadata (authors, keywords, classifiers, project URLs)
